title: Chapter Ninety-Two: The Arsenal
content:
## JEONG YEON-SHIN

A hearty laugh spread across Se-jin's face. "Truly magnificent. I've lost once."

"You showed the momentum of Emperor Sword Form. I too gained something."

"You *gained* something?" Se-jin's smile deepened. "I'm curious how you'll unravel it."

Competitive spirit burned in his expression. Yeon-shin read it clearly. The appearance of one walking the martial path.

*Azure Qilin Namgung Se-jin. What a peculiar person.*

He deliberately emptied his mind. There was room, on a short journey like this, for companionship to sprout even with a life-and-death opponent. That could not happen.

They walked on. Summer rain began falling.

Yeon-shin walked silently in it and newly realized something: he had become numb to killing. He felt nothing at all. Just cut down enemies, that was all. It wasn't like this at the Jeong family estate, when he had to look away from his first kill.

*Was it because I am chased by time? Or because my heart wants to live, even if it means stepping on corpses?*

The raindrops hitting his shoulders were cold.

---

Far to the west, in Desolate Fortress' Heavenly Gold Arsenal, a girl in white martial robes stood before classically arranged bookshelves.

Shin So-bin. Radiant Demon Wing's White Cat -- a title she despised.

"What are you looking for?"

She turned to find Red Day Sword -- a middle-aged warrior scarred across face and neck, built like he had survived a hundred battles.

"Senior Lightning Genius' martial arts manuals," she said. "But they're all gone."

"Of course they are. It's the youngest blue-rank master's martial arts. Everyone's interested." Red Day Sword's voice held something like reverence. "Fate Defying Scripture, Radiant Wing Step, Demon Destroying Azure Unyielding Palm, Eternal Blossom Fist, Radiant Sword Style -- he donated everything. Secret manuals worthy of discussing all martial arts under heaven. Black-ranked sect leaders have come to study them."

"There was much debate in Radiant Demon Wing about whether it was wasteful."

"Rightly so. Martial arts that spread widely inevitably have counter-techniques developed. Even excluding Demon Destroying Azure Unyielding Palm, which no one could enter -- these were Lightning Genius' main techniques. Even with achievements urgent, it's hard to understand."

So-bin raised her head and smirked. "He said he wants to try breaking the counters. That achievements would advance greatly. Even if I mastered the first three moves of Eternal Blossom Fist and stood before him, he'd calmly bring out the fourth."

*Breaking the counters to the counters.* Red Day Sword seemed at a loss for words.

"I didn't know I had so many competitors," So-bin said. "Fortress warriors entering his martial arts in droves. He's becoming a sect founder."

"He can't be called that -- no sect established. But a legacy of Lightning Genius is forming." Red Day Sword spoke slowly. "The title *Great Master* would be fitting."

---
